purpose: LITE worker-agent progress-reporting protocol for simple, independent tasks (no upstream dependencies). Streamlined alternative to tracker_worker_instructions.md. Defines the {tracker_root} vs {project_root} key-paths distinction, plan.json task-spec read requirement, the progress-file path and minimal JSON schema with all 10 fields (task_id, status, started_at/completed_at, summary, assigned_agent, stage, message, milestones, deviations, logs, optional annotations), the seven fixed stages, the FIVE mandatory writes (vs FULL's 8 — no upstream-deps step, no files_changed enforcement, no shared_context), live-timestamp rule, optional PKI annotations rule, and the structured return format. Used when the master selects INSTRUCTION MODE: LITE for a worker.
gotchas: LITE is for simple INDEPENDENT tasks only — for dependent or complex tasks, use the FULL tracker_worker_instructions.md instead.

Workers reading LITE vs FULL get DIFFERENT mandatory-write counts (5 vs 8) and DIFFERENT progress-file requirements — confirm which mode the master assigned via the dispatch prompt's INSTRUCTION MODE field.
LITE does NOT enforce files_changed[] (FULL does) — but you should still populate it if your task creates/modifies/deletes files for dashboard rendering.
Always use the Write tool, not shell echo/cat — even in LITE mode.
Worker is sole owner of the progress file — write the FULL file every time, no read-modify-write.
Live timestamps only — `date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"`. Never guess or hardcode.
plan.json is the source of truth — your dispatch prompt summarizes the task entry and shared `context`, but read plan.json directly before doing anything else.
Deviation severity classifications still apply in LITE: CRITICAL (affects downstream), MODERATE (different approach, same outcome), MINOR (cosmetic).
PKI annotations are OPTIONAL — only annotate files you actually read deeply, don't speculate.
patterns: Compressed schema documentation — single JSON example with field-name | type | description table.
Five mandatory writes (vs FULL's eight): before starting work, on every stage transition, on any deviation, on completion, on failure.
Same fixed stages as FULL: reading_context → planning → implementing → testing → finalizing → completed | failed.
Same return format as FULL: STATUS / SUMMARY / FILES CHANGED / EXPORTS / DIVERGENT ACTIONS / WARNINGS / ERRORS — with omit-if-none rule for optional sections.
Same annotations schema as FULL but emphasized as 'Optional'.
conventions: Field-name | Type | Description table for the progress-file schema.
Stage | Description table for the fixed stages.
Numbered Mandatory Writes list, terse one-line each.

depended_by: agent/_commands/p_track_execution.md (Step 14 worker prompt template — INSTRUCTION MODE: LITE)
